_comment: Pinned source versions so builds are reproducible WITHOUT committing the corpora (which would bloat clones — Orayta alone is ~300MB). The fetched data lives in ./data (gitignored); adapters fetch from the refs below. Update a ref here to move a source to a new version.

sefaria.note: FULL corpus: every book's merged Hebrew + English fetched from this GCS bucket (mutable, ~monthly). indexRef pins the Sefaria-Export books.json snapshot as a version marker; the bucket itself is not content-addressable.

sefaria-links.note: The links/ CSV export (18 shards, ~657MB) — the full cross-reference graph. Same bucket/version marker as sefaria.

opensiddur.note: Open Siddur Project source texts (whole-siddurim). We ingest only the plain-text, public-domain siddurim (Rambam Seder ha-Tefilot, Singer Standard Prayer Book).

dicta-talmud.note: Vocalized Babylonian Talmud (CC BY-SA, Dicta/Israel). ktiv-haser .txt per tractate; FULL Shas (37 tractates, auto-discovered from the repo tree).

dicta-library.note: Per-book OCR text ZIPs (CC BY-SA 4.0, Dicta). URLs are mutable (no content hash); subset: achiezer, levushmordechaigittin.

hewikisource.note: Biur (modern Hebrew Tanakh commentary), CC BY-SA 4.0. Fetched via the API at current revision (mutable); subset: Biur on Genesis/Psalms/Deuteronomy.
